text: A three-centimeter incision is made in the right lower quadrant, with a disc-shaped section of skin excised. The surgeon enters the abdomen through a muscle-splitting incision, establishing pneumoperitoneum after inserting the single port gel point system.
The surgeon inspects the small bowel for any abnormalities before identifying the right colon. The iliacolic pedicle is then divided, marking the beginning of the colectomy procedure.

The surgeon performs medial to lateral separation of the right colon in a cephalad direction, extending up to the transverse colon. The lateral attachments of the colon are taken down, followed by mobilization of the hepatic flexure and terminal ileum.

The surgeon dissects the omentum from the transverse colon and divides the middle colic vessels. The duodenum is identified and preserved, while the splenic flexure is mobilized, extending the dissection down to the left colon.
The surgeon mobilizes the rectosigmoid colon and staples across the rectosigmoid junction in the distal sigmoid area, leaving the well-vascularized segment in the pelvis.
The surgeon exteriorizes the stapled end of the colon specimen through the right lower quadrant stoma site. The area is narrowed using zero polysorb sutures, and the stoma is matured in place.
